channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: CNN founder Ted Turner dies
item.description: Ted Turner — the founder of CNN and a suite of other cable channels, not to mention a restaurant chain, anti-nuclear nonprofit and sports competition — has died.
item.content:encoded: Ted Turner — the founder of CNN and a suite of other cable channels, not to mention a restaurant chain, anti-nuclear nonprofit and sports competition — has died.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: The show still goes on: Federal employees are honored after a tumultuous year
item.description: Amid tumultuous times for federal workers, a team of USDA researchers is being honored with the Service to America medal. Their work over decades has helped to revolutionize the dairy industry.
item.content:encoded: Amid tumultuous times for federal workers, a team of USDA researchers is being honored with the Service to America medal. Their work over decades has helped to revolutionize the dairy industry.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Alcohol warning labels that link drinking to cancer are effective, study says
item.description: A new study finds updated warning labels pointing out the increased risks of specific diseases, like cancer, would do a better job of encouraging people to drink less alcohol than the current warning.
item.content:encoded: A new study finds updated warning labels pointing out the increased risks of specific diseases, like cancer, would do a better job of encouraging people to drink less alcohol than the current warning.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: Pope Leo's first year: foreign trips and tensions with Trump
item.description: In his first year, Pope Leo has spoken in favor of peace and the plight of immigrants, putting him in the crosshairs of the Trump administration. He meets with the U.S. secretary of state on Thursday.
item.content:encoded: In his first year, Pope Leo has spoken in favor of peace and the plight of immigrants, putting him in the crosshairs of the Trump administration. He meets with the U.S. secretary of state on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Tennessee joins southern push to redistricting in favor of the GOP
item.description: Over protests in the capitol, Tennessee lawmakers joined the rush of southern Republicans to redraw congressional voting maps now that protections for minority voting power have been weakened.
item.content:encoded: Over protests in the capitol, Tennessee lawmakers joined the rush of southern Republicans to redraw congressional voting maps now that protections for minority voting power have been weakened.
item.dc:creator: Marianna Bacallao

item.title: New Mexico opens investigations into possible crimes at Epstein's old ranch
item.description: New Mexico officials have re-opened an old criminal investigation and formed a legislative committee to look into allegations of abuse at the 10,000-acre ranch owned by Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded: New Mexico officials have re-opened an old criminal investigation and formed a legislative committee to look into allegations of abuse at the 10,000-acre ranch owned by Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL MONTANO

item.title: The Trump administration's changing Iran policy
item.description: It's been a week of shifting messages and strategies on the Iran war. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly breaks down the week with Pentagon correspondent Tom Bowman.
item.content:encoded: It's been a week of shifting messages and strategies on the Iran war. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly breaks down the week with Pentagon correspondent Tom Bowman.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: A new underground map of the U.S. could help the electric grid avoid catastrophe
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Paul Bedrosian of the U.S. Geological Survey about a new map and model of the deep continental structure underneath the United States.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Paul Bedrosian of the U.S. Geological Survey about a new map and model of the deep continental structure underneath the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Geiran

item.title: ICE is giving local police big money to help with immigration enforcement
item.description: The government is offering local police big incentives if they participate in a program that allows their officers to arrest undocumented immigrants. But there's a lot that's unknown about this money.
item.content:encoded: The government is offering local police big incentives if they participate in a program that allows their officers to arrest undocumented immigrants. But there's a lot that's unknown about this money.
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: Is hantavirus the next COVID? Is the U.S. response on point? An outbreak update
item.description: The outbreak began in early April on a cruise ship. Now health authorities around the world are working to contain it. Here's what infectious disease experts have to say.
item.content:encoded:
The outbreak began in early April on a cruise ship. Now health authorities around the world are working to contain it. Here's what infectious disease experts have to say.

(Image credit: Alfred Pasieka/Science Photo Library)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel
